explanations.6: The 'exec' command replaces the current shell process with the docker command, so when docker finishes, the script exits immediately instead of continuing.
explanations.7: The ':cached' suffix tells Docker to use a performance optimization for this mount on Mac, which trades perfect file consistency for speed since we're only reading files.
explanations.13: This substitutes the current user's numeric ID and group ID so the container runs as the same user instead of root, which is a security practice.
